refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable measurement and reporting of energy consumption per unit of work output.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) the ability to measure and report energy efficiency metrics, and (2) the achievement of a target value. The latter is a performance constraint or acceptance criterion, not a functional requirement of the system itself. The system's functional role is to provide visibility into energy consumption and work output so that operators, managers, and engineers can assess performance against targets. Achievement of the target is a validation outcome dependent on operational practices, equipment tuning, and worksite conditions—not solely a system design obligation. By refining to measurement and reporting, the FR becomes solution-neutral (does not prescribe how energy is reduced) and atomic (single, testable obligation).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the NGHE solution captures and reports energy_used (kWh or equivalent) and ton_moved (or equivalent work unit) over defined evaluation periods, with sufficient granularity and accuracy to support calculation of energy_per_ton. Confirm that reported metrics are accessible to authorized stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance) via defined interfaces (dashboard, API, logs).
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall measure and log energy consumption (kWh) during operation.
FR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall measure and log work output (tons moved or equivalent unit) during operation.
FR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall calculate and report the energy efficiency metric (energy_per_ton) over user-defined evaluation periods.
FR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide access to energy efficiency reports to authorized stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Instrument energy input (fuel/electricity) at source; instrument work output (tonnage moved) at point of delivery; compute ratio offline post-evaluation period
DP2: Deploy real-time energy and tonnage sensors with continuous data aggregation; compute energy_per_ton at configurable intervals; expose metric via reporting interface

DP3: Integrate energy metering into power distribution subsystem; integrate tonnage measurement into load/transport subsystem; synchronize timestamps and compute ratio in a dedicated metering logic module
DP4: Establish energy accounting via fuel consumption logs and work logs (manual or semi-automated); reconcile and compute energy_per_ton through periodic audit process

DP5: Decompose into two independent measurement streams—(a) energy consumption measurement with configurable granularity, (b) work output quantification with independent sensor/data path—unified only at reporting layer via time-windowed aggregation
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Decompose into two independent measurement streams—(a) energy consumption measurement with configurable granularity, (b) work output quantification with independent sensor/data path—unified only at reporting layer via time-windowed aggregation

potential_sources: Timestamp synchronization between energy and tonnage streams (mitigated by loose coupling at reporting layer)
Shared data infrastructure (acceptable if interfaces are well-defined)
Dependency on common time reference (acceptable; does not violate functional independence)
coupling_check.notes: DP5 achieves functional independence by keeping energy measurement and work output measurement as separate logical concerns, each with its own sensor/data acquisition path. Coupling occurs only at the reporting/aggregation layer, which is a natural and low-risk integration point. This design avoids the tight coupling of DP3 (where metering logic is embedded in subsystems) and the offline-only limitation of DP1. DP2 and DP4 are viable but DP2 introduces unnecessary real-time computation overhead, and DP4 lacks precision for acceptance criteria validation.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Energy source and work output can be independently instrumented without major architectural disruption. Risk: If worksite equipment is legacy/non-instrumented, retrofit cost may be prohibitive. Mitigation: Phased instrumentation or hybrid manual/sensor approach.

Assumption: Time synchronization between energy and tonnage data streams is achievable (e.g., via GPS, NTP, or local clock). Risk: Clock drift or network latency could introduce systematic error in ratio computation. Mitigation: Implement clock validation and time-window tolerance in reporting logic.

Assumption: Energy consumption and work output are measurable at distinct physical/logical points. Risk: If equipment design couples energy input to work output tightly (e.g., single integrated sensor), DP5 may require additional instrumentation. Mitigation: Conduct early feasibility study on sensor placement.

Assumption: Acceptance criteria `energy_per_ton ≤ E_target` is evaluated over a defined period with stable operating conditions. Risk: Variability in worksite conditions (terrain, load type, operator skill) may cause ratio to fluctuate; unclear if target is a mean, percentile, or worst-case. Mitigation: Clarify acceptance criteria definition (e.g., rolling average, 95th percentile) before implementation.

Assumption: Reporting interface can tolerate minor latency (e.g., data available within hours, not real-time). Risk: If operational decisions require sub-minute feedback, DP5's post-aggregation approach may be insufficient. Mitigation: Add optional real-time dashboard if needed, but keep core acceptance criterion tied to post-evaluation aggregation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR (refined): The NGHE solution shall enable independent measurement of energy consumption and work output, with time-synchronized aggregation to compute and report energy_per_ton = energy_used / ton_moved, such that the ratio satisfies ≤ E_target when evaluated over the agreed evaluation period under defined worksite operating conditions. Acceptance criteria shall specify whether E_target is a mean, percentile, or worst-case threshold, and shall account for expected variability in worksite conditions (terrain, load type, operator). Implementation shall ensure timestamp synchronization tolerance (e.g., ±5 min) and shall document sensor placement, calibration, and data quality assurance procedures to support audit and validation of reported metrics.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Unified measurement and reporting system encompassing dual independent measurement streams (energy consumption and work output) with time-windowed aggregation at reporting layer

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exports aggregated energy and work output metrics via time-windowed reporting; carries unified measurement results to external consumers

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task directives and granularity configuration parameters for energy consumption measurement stream

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides communication substrate for dual measurement streams to operate independently; carries sensor data and aggregation signals

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment: NGHE Solution is the natural home for measurement decomposition and unified reporting. Both measurement streams (energy and work output) logically belong to a holistic energy/performance solution. Time-windowed aggregation at reporting layer is a core NGHE responsibility. Minimal diffusion: only Telemetry export and Tasking input cross boundaries; internal stream independence is preserved within module.

Poor containment: Vehicle module is too low-level and hardware-centric. Measurement decomposition and aggregation logic are solution-level concerns, not vehicle-level. Would require Vehicle to own reporting layer, creating tight coupling between vehicle hardware and measurement strategy. Energy and work output streams would diffuse across vehicle subsystems and NGHE Solution.

Very poor containment: Teleoperation is a control/command interface, not a measurement aggregation system. Placing dual measurement streams and time-windowed reporting here violates separation of concerns. Would create high diffusion: measurement logic would leak into command handling, and reporting would require bidirectional coupling with Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

Poor containment: Safety Services is orthogonal to measurement decomposition. While safety may consume measurement outputs, owning the dual measurement streams and aggregation logic here conflates safety monitoring with energy/performance measurement. High diffusion: would require Safety Services to manage non-safety measurement concerns and couple tightly with Vehicle and reporting consumers.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP5 (dual independent measurement streams with unified time-windowed aggregation) is fundamentally a solution-level measurement architecture concern. NGHE Solution is the appropriate boundary because: (1) it owns the holistic energy and performance measurement strategy; (2) it naturally integrates energy consumption and work output as complementary metrics; (3) time-windowed aggregation is a reporting-layer responsibility that NGHE Solution controls; (4) independent stream design is preserved within the module boundary, minimizing internal coupling; (5) external interfaces (Telemetry for output, Tasking for configuration, Network Link for substrate) are all natural NGHE Solution touchpoints.
containment_risks: Risk: If Vehicle subsystems require direct access to raw measurement streams (not aggregated), additional internal interfaces may be needed, potentially increasing coupling within NGHE Solution.

Risk: Configurable granularity for energy consumption (via Tasking) must be clearly specified to avoid ambiguity about which module owns granularity policy—recommend explicit DP refinement on granularity ownership.
Risk: Time-windowing parameters (window size, aggregation frequency) must be owned and managed within NGHE Solution; if external modules attempt to override these, containment will degrade.
Risk: Sensor/data path independence for work output requires clear interface contracts; if Vehicle or other modules bypass the independent path, diffusion will increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 directly addresses the FR requirement to enable measurement and reporting of energy consumption per unit of work output. The decomposition into two independent measurement streams (energy consumption and work output) with unified time-windowed aggregation at the reporting layer is a sound logical solution that preserves measurement independence while enabling the required ratio calculation (energy_per_ton = energy_used / ton_moved). The DP is sufficiently detailed to guide implementation and is traceable to the acceptance criterion.

diffusion_risks: Granularity configuration ownership: Tasking interface delivers granularity parameters to energy consumption stream, but the DP does not explicitly specify whether granularity policy (e.g., per-cycle vs. per-minute vs. per-task) is owned by NGHE Solution or negotiated with external tasking authority. Recommend explicit DP refinement to clarify granularity ownership and prevent diffusion of policy logic.

Raw measurement stream access: If Vehicle subsystems or Safety Services require direct access to un-aggregated energy or work output data (e.g., for real-time safety thresholds), additional internal interfaces within NGHE Solution will be needed. Current DP assumes aggregation-only export via Telemetry; deviation from this assumption increases coupling and diffusion risk.

Time-windowing parameter management: Window size and aggregation frequency are critical to the DP but are not explicitly assigned to a responsible module. If these parameters are externally configurable (e.g., via Tasking or Teleoperation), NGHE Solution must enforce strict contracts to prevent external override, which could degrade measurement integrity.

Work output sensor/data path independence: The DP specifies independent sensor/data path for work output, but does not detail how Vehicle subsystems (e.g., odometry, load sensors) interface with this path. If Vehicle modules bypass the independent path or duplicate work output logic, diffusion will increase. Recommend explicit interface specification.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct ownership boundary with a containment score of 0.92 and diffusion score of 0.08. The module naturally owns the holistic measurement strategy, integrates complementary metrics (energy and work output), and controls the reporting layer. External interfaces (Telemetry out, Tasking in, Network Link inout) are appropriate and minimal. Internal stream independence is preserved. However, containment can be strengthened by: (1) explicitly assigning granularity policy ownership to NGHE Solution, (2) clarifying which modules may access raw measurement streams and under what conditions, (3) centralizing time-windowing parameter management within NGHE Solution with read-only external visibility, and (4) formalizing the work output sensor/data path interface contract to prevent Vehicle subsystem diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The acceptance criterion (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target over evaluation period) is directly verifiable from the dual measurement streams unified at the reporting layer. NGHE Solution's Telemetry interface exports aggregated energy and work output metrics, enabling external validation of the ratio. Time-windowed aggregation provides clear temporal boundaries for measurement collection and reporting. Verification requires: (1) instrumentation of energy consumption measurement with configurable granularity, (2) independent work output quantification via sensor/data path, (3) time-windowed aggregation logic with documented window parameters, and (4) Telemetry export of aggregated results. All are within NGHE Solution's scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Good, with minor gaps. The FR is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution as the owning module. The DP is logically sound and traceable to the FR. However, allocation clarity could be improved by: (1) explicitly documenting which Vehicle subsystems provide sensor inputs to the work output measurement stream and under what interface contracts, (2) specifying the Tasking interface contract for granularity configuration (e.g., valid granularity values, update frequency, precedence rules), (3) defining time-windowing parameters (window size, aggregation frequency, overlap policy) as NGHE Solution-owned constants with external read-only visibility, and (4) clarifying whether Safety Services or other modules may subscribe to raw measurement streams or only aggregated Telemetry output. These refinements will eliminate ambiguity and prevent diffusion during detailed design.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP5 to explicitly assign granularity policy ownership to NGHE Solution. Specify valid granularity values (e.g., per-cycle, per-minute, per-task) and document how Tasking configuration parameters map to internal granularity settings. Include a constraint that granularity changes do not invalidate in-flight aggregation windows.

Add a sub-DP for work output sensor/data path independence: specify which Vehicle subsystems (e.g., odometry, load sensors) feed the work output measurement stream, define the interface contract (data format, update frequency, latency bounds), and establish a rule that Vehicle subsystems must not duplicate work output logic or bypass the independent path.

Add a sub-DP for time-windowing parameter management: centralize window size, aggregation frequency, and overlap policy as NGHE Solution-owned configuration. Document whether these parameters are static (design-time) or dynamic (runtime-configurable via Tasking), and if dynamic, specify update semantics (e.g., window-boundary-aligned changes only).

Clarify the Telemetry interface contract: specify the structure and frequency of aggregated energy and work output metrics, the time-window metadata included in each report (start time, end time, window duration), and whether raw measurement streams are accessible to external consumers or only aggregated results.

Add a containment rule: Vehicle subsystems and Safety Services may consume aggregated Telemetry output but must not access raw energy or work output measurement streams directly. If real-time safety thresholds require raw data, define a separate safety-specific interface within NGHE Solution with explicit latency and accuracy guarantees.

Document the evaluation period and time-windowing alignment: specify how the agreed evaluation period (from acceptance criteria) maps to time-windowed aggregation. Clarify whether the evaluation period is a multiple of the aggregation window size and how partial windows at period boundaries are handled (e.g., excluded, pro-rated, or carried forward).

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass, but the DP-IM gate identifies six material diffusion risks and containment gaps that must be resolved before proceeding to sub-FR decomposition. Given high safety criticality, unresolved granularity ownership, raw measurement stream access ambiguity, time-windowing parameter management, work output sensor interface clarity, and evaluation period alignment are unacceptable risks to carry forward. The recommended revisions are structural (not cosmetic) and affect module boundaries, interface contracts, and safety-critical measurement integrity. Revising DP5 and adding sub-DPs now prevents downstream rework and ensures containment is locked before detailed design. Proceeding to sub-FRs without these revisions would propagate ambiguity into lower levels and increase diffusion during implementation.

revise.why: DP5 must be refined to explicitly address: (1) granularity policy ownership and Tasking interface contract, (2) work output sensor/data path independence with Vehicle subsystem interface rules, (3) time-windowing parameter management (centralized, static or dynamic, update semantics), (4) Telemetry interface contract (aggregated metrics structure, frequency, window metadata, raw stream access rules), (5) containment rule preventing Vehicle and Safety Services from accessing raw measurement streams, and (6) evaluation period alignment with time-windowed aggregation. These refinements are necessary to eliminate diffusion risks and clarify module boundaries before sub-FR decomposition. The DP logic is sound; the revisions strengthen ownership clarity and interface contracts.

assumptions: Granularity policy (per-cycle, per-minute, per-task) is owned by NGHE Solution, not negotiated with external Tasking authority.

Work output measurement stream receives sensor inputs from Vehicle subsystems (odometry, load sensors) via a formal interface contract that prevents Vehicle modules from duplicating work output logic.

Time-windowing parameters (window size, aggregation frequency, overlap policy) are NGHE Solution-owned configuration, either static (design-time) or dynamic (runtime-configurable via Tasking with explicit update semantics).

Aggregated energy and work output metrics are exported via Telemetry interface; raw measurement streams are not directly accessible to external consumers (Vehicle subsystems, Safety Services, Teleoperation).

Safety Services and other modules may consume aggregated Telemetry output but must not access raw energy or work output data. If real-time safety thresholds require raw data, a separate safety-specific interface within NGHE Solution is defined with explicit latency and accuracy guarantees.

Evaluation period (from acceptance criteria) is aligned with time-windowed aggregation; evaluation period is a multiple of aggregation window size, and partial windows at period boundaries are handled consistently (excluded, pro-rated, or carried forward).
open_risks: Granularity ownership diffusion: If Tasking authority can override granularity policy without NGHE Solution consent, measurement integrity and reporting consistency are compromised. Mitigation: Explicit DP refinement assigning granularity ownership to NGHE Solution with read-only Tasking visibility.

Raw measurement stream access diffusion: If Vehicle subsystems or Safety Services bypass aggregation and access raw energy or work output data, coupling increases and measurement independence is lost. Mitigation: Containment rule enforcing aggregated-only export via Telemetry; safety-specific interface defined separately if needed.

Time-windowing parameter management diffusion: If window size and aggregation frequency are externally configurable without NGHE Solution control, measurement windows may be invalidated or inconsistently applied. Mitigation: Centralize time-windowing parameters as NGHE Solution-owned configuration with explicit update semantics.

Work output sensor interface ambiguity: If Vehicle subsystems (odometry, load sensors) interface with work output measurement stream without formal contract, duplication and inconsistency risk increases. Mitigation: Explicit interface specification with data format, update frequency, latency bounds, and rule preventing Vehicle module duplication.

Evaluation period misalignment: If evaluation period does not align with time-windowed aggregation boundaries, acceptance criterion verification becomes ambiguous (partial windows, pro-rating rules unclear). Mitigation: Document evaluation period alignment and partial-window handling rules in revised DP.

Safety-critical measurement integrity: High safety criticality requires that energy consumption and work output measurements are accurate, tamper-proof, and auditable. Current DP does not address measurement validation, error detection, or safety-specific access controls. Mitigation: Add sub-DP for measurement validation and safety-specific interface if real-time thresholds are required.
evidence_needed: Refined DP5 with explicit granularity policy ownership, Tasking interface contract (valid granularity values, update frequency, precedence rules), and constraint that granularity changes do not invalidate in-flight aggregation windows.

Sub-DP for work output sensor/data path independence: specification of Vehicle subsystems (odometry, load sensors) feeding the measurement stream, interface contract (data format, update frequency, latency bounds), and rule preventing Vehicle module duplication.

Sub-DP for time-windowing parameter management: centralized ownership of window size, aggregation frequency, overlap policy; documentation of static vs. dynamic configuration; if dynamic, explicit update semantics (e.g., window-boundary-aligned changes only).

Telemetry interface contract specification: structure and frequency of aggregated energy and work output metrics, time-window metadata (start time, end time, duration), and explicit rule that raw measurement streams are not accessible to external consumers.

Containment rule documentation: Vehicle subsystems and Safety Services may consume aggregated Telemetry output only; raw energy or work output streams are not accessible. If real-time safety thresholds require raw data, define separate safety-specific interface within NGHE Solution with explicit latency and accuracy guarantees.

Evaluation period alignment specification: mapping of agreed evaluation period to time-windowed aggregation; confirmation that evaluation period is a multiple of aggregation window size; documented handling of partial windows at period boundaries (excluded, pro-rated, or carried forward).

Measurement validation and error detection strategy: given high safety criticality, evidence of how measurement accuracy, tamper-proofing, and auditability are ensured (e.g., checksums, redundancy, logging).
